content.text:
Now I have a clear 3D view of the molecule with all atom labels visible. Let me examine atom index 9 (S:9) more carefully to identify all its neighbor atoms.

From the image, I can clearly see:
- Atom 9 is Sulfur (S:9) - shown as a yellow/gold sphere
- It is directly bonded to:
  - Atom 10 (O:10) - Oxygen (red sphere) - connected by a bond
  - Atom 11 (O:11) - Oxygen (red sphere) - connected by a bond  
  - Atom 12 (N:12) - Nitrogen (blue sphere) - connected by a bond
  - Atom 4 (C:4) - Carbon (yellow/gold sphere) - connected by a bond

The sulfur atom is in a tetrahedral arrangement with four covalent bonds: two to oxygen atoms (10 and 11), one to nitrogen (12), and one to carbon (4). This confirms it's a sulfonamide group structure.

Based on the file name "geomconf_3610.xyz", the geomconf_index is 3610.

The neighbor atoms of atom 9 (S) are:
- Atom 10 (O)
- Atom 11 (O)
- Atom 12 (N)
- Atom 4 (C)

I have confirmed these connections from multiple viewing angles, and there are no other atoms directly bonded to the sulfur atom at index 9.

Answer: The neighbor atoms of atom 9 (S) are: atom 10 (O), atom 11 (O), atom 12 (N), and atom 4 (C). The geomconf_index is 3610.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 9, whose element should be S. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
